authorityLabels: United States v. California Mobile Home Park Management Co.

authorityDetails.label: United States v. California Mobile Home Park Management Co.
authorityDetails.excerptText: The FHAA imposes an affirmative duty upon landlords reasonably to accommodate the needs of handicapped persons.
authorityDetails.proposition: Reasonable accommodation may require landlords to shoulder reasonable financial burdens.

authorityDetails.label: 24 CFR 982.316
authorityDetails.excerptText: The PHA must approve a live-in aide if needed as a reasonable accommodation.
authorityDetails.proposition: A PHA must approve a live-in aide when needed as a reasonable accommodation.

authorityDetails.label: Giebeler v. M&B Associates
authorityDetails.excerptText: Imposition of burdensome policies can interfere with disabled persons' right to use and enjoyment of their dwellings.
authorityDetails.proposition: Burdensome housing policies may require accommodation when they interfere with use and enjoyment.

authorityDetails.excerptText: Imposition of burdensome policies can interfere with disabled persons' right to use and enjoyment of their dwellings.
authorityDetails.proposition: Burdensome housing policies may require accommodation when they interfere with use and enjoyment.

authorityDetails.label: HUD/DOJ Joint Statement on Reasonable Accommodations
authorityDetails.excerptText: Reasonable accommodations may be necessary to afford a person with a disability equal opportunity to use and enjoy a dwelling.
authorityDetails.proposition: Equal use and enjoyment may require necessary accommodation changes.

authorityDetails.label: McGary v. City of Portland
authorityDetails.excerptText: The lien the City put on McGary's house prevents the full use and enjoyment of his property.
authorityDetails.proposition: Housing-related financial burdens can impair the full use and enjoyment of a dwelling.

texts.hearing_script:
Case: live_in_aide_case_001
Requested remedy: [Approve a separate bedroom for the live-in aide]
Requesting party: [Requesting Party]

Opening:
This case asks whether the approved live-in aide accommodation is effective in practice.
The evaluated record supports constructive denial because the aide was approved in principle but denied the separate bedroom needed to function.
Authority grounding note: All currently attached authority support is marked as verified_quote.
Citations: findings=dutyToGrant, constructiveDenial, violation; authorities=United States v. California Mobile Home Park Management Co., 24 CFR 982.316, Giebeler v. M&B Associates; evidence=evidence:medical_verification, evidence:sleep_log_1; events=evt:t1, evt:t2, evt:t3, evt:t4, evt:t5

Core points:
- The aide's sleep is disrupted because the sleeping space is shared and used at night.
- The shared sleeping arrangement interferes with the aide's remote software work.
- Fatigue from the arrangement undermines caregiving effectiveness.
- The arrangement creates a meaningful privacy loss within the home.
- Supporting authorities for the accommodation duty include: Giebeler v. M&B Associates, United States v. California Mobile Home Park Management Co., 24 CFR 982.316.

Closing:
An accommodation that exists on paper but fails in practice is not an effective accommodation.
texts.complaint_outline:
Case: live_in_aide_case_001
Requested remedy: [Approve a separate bedroom for the live-in aide]
Requesting party: [Requesting Party]

Complaint Outline For live_in_aide_case_001

Claim:
Failure to provide a reasonable accommodation under the Fair Housing Act.
Authority grounding note: All currently attached authority support is marked as verified_quote.
Citations: findings=necessary, reasonable, constructiveDenial, violation; authorities=United States v. California Mobile Home Park Management Co., 24 CFR 982.316, Giebeler v. M&B Associates, HUD/DOJ Joint Statement on Reasonable Accommodations, McGary v. City of Portland; evidence=evidence:layout_diagram_1, evidence:medical_verification, evidence:sleep_log_1; events=evt:t1, evt:t2, evt:t3, evt:t4, evt:t5

Core allegations:
- The tenant is disabled and needs a live-in aide.
- Medical verification supports the requested accommodation.
- A separate bedroom was requested for the aide.
- The housing provider approved the aide in principle but denied the separate bedroom.
- The aide's sleep is disrupted because the sleeping space is shared and used at night.
- The shared sleeping arrangement interferes with the aide's remote software work.
- Fatigue from the arrangement undermines caregiving effectiveness.
- The arrangement creates a meaningful privacy loss within the home.

Theory:
The accepted findings support a constructive denial theory because the accommodation was nominally recognized but denied in effective form.

Authorities to cite: Giebeler v. M&B Associates, United States v. California Mobile Home Park Management Co., 24 CFR 982.316.
texts.demand_letter:
Subject: Demand to Cure Constructive Denial of Live-In Aide Accommodation

To: [Housing Authority or Decision-Maker]

From: [Requesting Party]
Requested remedy: [Approve a separate bedroom for the live-in aide]
Requested response deadline: [10 business days]

This letter requests prompt approval of an accommodation that is effective in practice, not merely nominal.
The current evaluated record supports the conclusion that the separate-bedroom denial amounts to constructive denial of accommodation.
Authority grounding note: All currently attached authority support is marked as verified_quote.
Citations: findings=constructiveDenial, violation; authorities=United States v. California Mobile Home Park Management Co., 24 CFR 982.316, Giebeler v. M&B Associates; evidence=evidence:medical_verification, evidence:sleep_log_1; events=evt:t1, evt:t3, evt:t4, evt:t5
The present reasoning output identifies these harms: The aide's sleep is disrupted because the sleeping space is shared and used at night. The shared sleeping arrangement interferes with the aide's remote software work. Fatigue from the arrangement undermines caregiving effectiveness. The arrangement creates a meaningful privacy loss within the home.
Supporting authorities include Giebeler v. M&B Associates, United States v. California Mobile Home Park Management Co., 24 CFR 982.316.

Please confirm in writing whether the approved accommodation includes a separate bedroom for the live-in aide.
Please provide that confirmation by the requested deadline so this matter can be resolved without further escalation.

Sincerely,
[Requesting Party]
